title: 3B51Ravouq9Ez2Jrv0Yn9Fgjurgyy2
summary:
Main
For almost a decade now and year after year, analysts have predicted the death of coal as a fossil fuel, and as a result, the structural decline in global coal trading. And yet, although still unpredictable from year to year, over the past decade coal trading has seen strong periods of activity pushing dry bulk rates higher in the process. China remains the main driver when it comes to coal demand and trade, with roughly 10% of its annual consumption coming from imports.
